extension.valueMarkdown: In Arizona she had primary care and cardiology follow-up that established the chronic disease baseline: diabetes requiring intensification, CKD emerging, atrial fibrillation managed with anticoagulation, and HFrEF treated medically. After moving to New Mexico she had an acute decompensated heart-failure hospitalization, then longitudinal follow-up with a heart-failure clinic, nephrology, and retina specialists for diabetic retinopathy with macular edema.

coding.display: Hyperglycemia due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (disorder)

note.text: A1c increased to 9.4% and fasting glucose was 214 mg/dL during caregiving stress, missed meals, and inconsistent medication timing.
LDL 112 mg/dL and triglycerides 181 mg/dL. Atorvastatin increased to 40 mg nightly.
Blood pressure remained above goal at 154/92 mmHg, increasing concern in the setting of diabetes and CKD.

coding.display: Peripheral neuropathy due to diabetes mellitus (disorder)

code.text: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy of both feet

note.text: Burning foot pain, increasing toe numbness, diminished monofilament sensation at 6/10 points bilaterally, and reduced vibration sense were documented at this visit.
BMI 34.0 kg/m2. Counseled on diet and weight management.
Marked caregiving stress and grief symptoms were discussed; PHQ-2 was 2/6 and the patient denied self-harm thoughts.
Intact skin of both feet with no ulcers.
Dorsalis pedis pulses 2+ bilaterally.
Mildly reduced monofilament sensation at the plantar forefeet bilaterally.
No ulcers or recent injury reported.
Callus formation present at the metatarsal heads bilaterally.
Monofilament sensation diminished at 6/10 points bilaterally with reduced vibration sense at the great toes.
